summary: Fill M7 Beta scenario, person, and event content path
acceptance_criteria: Fill the contracted Beta scenario/person/event set through explicit content schemas and source labels; do not bypass schemas with object-graph serialization or implicit runtime data.
Every person, title, event, localization key, and scenario hook needed by the M7 contract has an owner, review status, stable ID, and deterministic ordering/tie-breaker where consumed by runtime.
The fill path records known gaps as BLOCKED, RESEARCH REQUIRED, or explicit post-lock defect/tuning candidates rather than hiding placeholders.
No package sim rules, client UI rules, save migration, release artifact, telemetry, networking, server, arbitrary-code mod, or manual node battle behavior is added.
Content compile and schema/runtime tests demonstrate that the filled path remains serializable, versionable, and compatible with accepted M1-M6 boundaries.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-SCENARIO-PERSON-EVENT-FILL-001
notes: M6_GATE remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS and must not be rewritten as unconditional PASS or release readiness.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE; this task must not create implementation, M7/M8 blocking spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency for manual node battle.
Player and AI behavior must continue through the same GameCommand/query/save surfaces where implementation work is later authorized.
sim-core must remain decoupled from React, Pixi, Electron, DOM, Node IO, real time APIs, hidden RPC, and object-graph serialization.
Stop and route to the named reviewer or Human Gate if allowed paths are insufficient, required tests cannot run, or a Human Gate decision is needed.
human_gates: manual playthrough acceptance where human judgment is required
commercial, release, store, branding, logo, or trademark decisions
telemetry, analytics, remote collection, accounts, paid services, or secrets
arbitrary-code mods, runtime scripting extensions, or Workshop support
cloud-save implementation, network persistence, or any account-backed save service
frozen product, platform, core architecture, technology stack, simulation authority, deterministic, or historical-policy changes
manual node battle reversal or any formal 1.0 scope reversal
scheduling_reason: Selected after M7-HISTORICAL-CULTURAL-LANGUAGE-REVIEW-001 because it is the direct content-lane consumer of the accepted review baseline; M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001 is also dependency-ready and remains unstarted.
next_action: historical_researcher writer must produce a REVIEW handoff routed to qa_reviewer after filling the contracted Beta scenario/person/event path and required tests.
